sources.title: Designing a scored rubric
sources.excerpt: A reliable LLM-as-judge scores each answer against an explicit rubric with named dimensions, not a single opaid score.

sources.title: Calibrating an LLM judge
sources.excerpt: The judge must be calibrated against human-labelled anchors before its scores can be trusted.

claims.text: A reliable LLM-as-judge scores against an explicit rubric with named dimensions.
The judge must be calibrated against human labels before its scores are trustworthy.

report: A reliable judge needs a rubric and calibration.

sources.title: Retrieval grounding
sources.excerpt: Grounding an answer in retrieved source excerpts reduces hallucination.

sources.title: Cite the source
sources.excerpt: Each claim should cite the retrieved source it rests on.

claims.text: Grounding the answer in retrieved source excerpts reduces hallucination.
Each claim must cite the retrieved source that supports it.

report: RAG grounds answers in retrieved sources and cites them.

sources.title: Multi-round research
sources.excerpt: A research run executes multiple rounds rather than a single search-and-summarize pass.

sources.title: Adversarial verification
sources.excerpt: An explicit adversarial verification step re-checks each claim against its sources.

claims.text: A research run executes multiple rounds, not a single pass.
An explicit adversarial verification step re-checks each claim.

report: Multi-round loop with an adversarial verification step.

sources.title: Per-run cost ceiling
sources.excerpt: A deep-research run needs a hard per-run cost ceiling because one action can burn real money.

sources.title: Cost attribution
sources.excerpt: Cost is attributed to the owner principal and surfaced in the trace.

claims.text: A deep-research run needs a hard cost ceiling because one action can burn real money.
Cost is attributed to the owner principal and made visible.

report: Cap declared in the registry entry's cost field, attributed to the owner.

sources.title: The golden set
sources.excerpt: A fixed golden question set is the re-runnable measurement baseline.

sources.title: Detecting regression
sources.excerpt: Scoring against the fixed set makes a quality regression detectable rather than felt.

claims.text: A fixed golden question set is the re-runnable baseline.
Scoring against it makes a quality regression detectable, not felt.

report: A golden set makes regression detectable.

sources.title: Sources on the canvas
sources.excerpt: Sources land as canvas nodes with no per-turn confirm widget.

sources.title: pmark disclosure
sources.excerpt: pmark 3-tier disclosure: hover costs 0 clicks, the panel costs 1 click.

claims.text: Sources land as canvas nodes without a per-turn confirm widget.
pmark disclosure means a hover costs 0 clicks and the panel costs 1.

report: Sources become canvas nodes; citations use pmark disclosure.
